title: 10 best herbal creams for jaundice

remedies.remedy_desc: Cassia auriculata creams helps with jaundice because of its potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties. The extract from Cassia auriculata, also known as 'Amukkuram', has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine to treat liver and digestive disorders. It is believed to enhance liver function, reduce bilirubin levels, and promote the excretion of waste products, thereby reducing the symptoms of jaundice. The cream's soothing and calming effects also help to comfort the skin and reduce itching associated with jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These compounds help with jaundice by reducing bilirubin levels in the blood and promoting its excretion, thus relieving symptoms associated with the condition.
Alkaloids: These compounds exhibit hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect the liver from damage and promoting its function in processing bilirubin, thus aiding in the treatment of jaundice.

Phenolic acids: These compounds have antioxidant properties, which help to neutralize free radicals that can damage liver cells and contribute to the development of jaundice, thereby aiding in its treatment.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Cassia auriculata leaves are used to make creams for jaundice due to their hepatoprotective and antioxidant properties.
Flowers: Cassia auriculata flowers are used to make creams for jaundice due to their potential to reduce liver inflammation and oxidative stress.
Barks: Cassia auriculata barks are used to make creams for jaundice due to their ability to stimulate liver function and improve bile flow.
remedy_recipe: Gather 100g of cassia auriculata leaves dried and powdered them in an electric grinder for 5 minutes.
Mix 100g of coconut oil 50g of beeswax and 10g of shea butter in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler at 180-200 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes stirring occasionally.
Add 20g of the powdered cassia auriculata leaves to the mixture and stir well for 10 minutes.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool and solidify completely before transferring to a container.

remedies.remedy_desc: Terminalia chebula creams helps with jaundice because of its hepatoprotective properties, which aid in liver health and function. The herb contains chebulinic acid, chebulagic acid, and other compounds that have been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder issues. Chebulinic acid has been shown to exhibit antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties, which help to reduce liver inflammation and promote bile flow, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms. This traditional herbal remedy has been used for centuries to support liver health and treat various liver-related disorders.
remedy_constituents: Chebulinic acid: Helps to reduce bilirubin levels and alleviate jaundice symptoms by its antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties.
Tannins: Exerts anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which can help in reducing liver inflammation and promoting the removal of bilirubin from the blood.

Gallic acid: Exhibits potent antioxidant and free radical scavenging activities, which can help in protecting the liver from oxidative damage and promoting its function in removing bilirubin from the blood.
remedy_parts: Fruits: The fruits of Terminalia chebula are used to make creams for jaundice due to their potential hepatoprotective properties.
Barks: The barks of Terminalia chebula are used to make creams for jaundice due to their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: The seeds of Terminalia chebula are used to make creams for jaundice due to their potential anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest terminalia chebula fruits from the mature tree and wash them thoroughly under cold running water for 10 minutes.
Dry the fruits in a single layer on paper towels for 5 days or until they become brittle and crumbly.
Grind the dried terminalia chebula fruits into a fine powder using a coffee grinder for 2 minutes.
Mix 5 grams of the powdered terminalia chebula with 20 grams of coconut oil in a double boiler for 30 minutes.
Stir the mixture well and let it cool down to room temperature before pouring it into a clean glass jar.

remedies.remedy_desc: Curcuma longa creams helps with jaundice because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties, which aid in reducing liver inflammation and promoting its natural healing process. The active compound, curcumin, in these creams has been shown to improve liver function, enhance bile production, and increase the conjugation of bilirubin, a key factor in jaundice. By reducing bilirubin levels and promoting liver health, Curcuma longa creams may help alleviate symptoms associated with jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes.
remedy_constituents: Curcumin: It has potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help in reducing liver inflammation and oxidative stress, thereby aiding in the treatment of jaundice.

Demethoxycurcumin (DMC): It exhibits strong antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities that help in protecting the liver from damage and promoting its recovery, thus helping to alleviate jaundice symptoms.

Bis-demethoxycurcumin (BDMC): It has been found to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help in reducing liver damage and promoting its regeneration, thus contributing to the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: They are the main part used due to their high curcumin content, which has potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help reduce jaundice symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Curcuma longa are also used as they contain bioactive compounds that help in the treatment of jaundice by reducing bilirubin levels and promoting liver health.
Seeds: Although less commonly used, the seeds of Curcuma longa may be used in some creams for their potential hepatoprotective properties, which can aid in the treatment of jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Weigh 200g of curcuma longa dried tubers and grind them into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 2 minutes.
Combine the ground curcuma longa powder with 50g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture in the double boiler for 10 minutes or until the beeswax is fully melted and the curcuma longa powder is well combined.
Remove the double boiler from the heat and let the mixture cool to around 40°c then whip it using a hand mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream into a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks or in the freezer for up to 6 months.

remedies.remedy_desc: Silybum marianum creams helps with jaundice because they contain silymarin, a bioactive compound that has hepatoprotective properties. Silymarin helps to regenerate liver cells, reduce oxidative stress, and improve liver function, which can aid in the reduction of jaundice symptoms. The anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of silymarin also help to alleviate the discomfort and inflammation associated with jaundice, making it a popular natural remedy for this condition. Regular use of silymarin creams may help to alleviate jaundice symptoms and promote overall liver health.
remedy_constituents: Silymarin: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory to reduce liver damage and promote liver cell regeneration, which helps alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Flavonoids: Help reduce liver inflammation and scarring, improve liver function, and protect liver cells from damage, contributing to the reduction of jaundice.
Fatty acids: Have anti-inflammatory properties that help reduce liver inflammation and damage, promoting liver health and reducing the severity of jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Seeds: Used to make creams for jaundice because they contain silymarin, a flavonoid complex that helps reduce bilirubin levels in the blood.
Leaves: Used to make creams for jaundice because they contain flavonoids and phenolic acids that have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.
Fruits: Used to make creams for jaundice because they contain saponins and flavonoids that help reduce inflammation and promote liver health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 250g of silybum marianum flowers and leaves, clean and dry them thoroughly in the shade for 2 hours.
Mix 100g of the dried silybum marianum with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a heat-proof bowl.
Melt the mixture in a double boiler over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally until smooth consistency.
Add 20g of distilled water to the mixture and continue stirring until the mixture reaches a creamy texture, within 10 minutes.
Pour the mixture into sterile containers, allow it to cool and solidify completely at room temperature for 2 hours.

remedies.remedy_desc: Glycyrrhiza glabra creams helps with jaundice because of its anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The licorice root extract in these creams has been traditionally used to soothe and protect the liver and gallbladder. It aids in the breakdown and excretion of bilirubin, a key compound responsible for the yellow discoloration associated with jaundice. By promoting liver function and reducing oxidative stress, Glycyrrhiza glabra creams may help alleviate jaundice symptoms and support overall liver health. Its potential benefits in this area are still being researched and explored.
remedy_constituents: Saponins: These compounds have been shown to improve liver function and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood, thus alleviating jaundice symptoms.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Glycyrrhiza glabra have antioxidant properties, which help reduce oxidative stress in the liver and improve its ability to metabolize bilirubin.
Glycyrrhizin: Glycyrrhizin has anti-inflammatory properties, which may help reduce liver inflammation associated with jaundice and promote overall liver health.
remedy_parts: Roots: They contain glycyrrhizin, a compound that has been traditionally used to treat liver and gallbladder disorders, including jaundice.
Leaves: They are used to make creams that help in reducing inflammation and soothing the skin, which can be beneficial for jaundice patients.
Barks: They contain flavonoids and other compounds that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can be used to make creams to treat jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Extract 1: harvest 20 grams of dried glycyrrhiza glabra roots and chop them into fine pieces for infusion.
Infuse 1: steep the chopped glycyrrhiza glabra roots in 100 milliliters of boiling water for 10 minutes at 95°f temperature.
Filter 1: strain the infusion through a cheesecloth to remove the solid particles from the liquid.
Mix 1: combine 20 grams of distilled water with 10 grams of glycerin and 5 grams of beeswax in a heat-resistant container.
Emulsify 1: blend the filtered infusion with the mixture of water glycerin and beeswax in a double boiler at 180°f temperature.

remedies.remedy_desc: Phyllanthus niruri creams helps with jaundice because of its unique properties that aid in the reduction of bilirubin levels in the blood. The herb is rich in flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have potent antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects. These compounds help in the breakdown of bilirubin, a key component in jaundice, and promote its elimination from the body. Additionally, Phyllanthus niruri has been traditionally used to support liver function and promote overall digestive health, making it a promising natural remedy for jaundice treatment.
remedy_constituents: Phenolic acids: Help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood by inhibiting the enzyme responsible for its production, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms.
Flavonoids: Exhibit antioxidant properties that protect liver cells from damage, promote liver regeneration, and reduce bilirubin levels, ultimately aiding in the recovery from jaundice.
Triterpenoids: Possess hepatoprotective properties, which help repair and regenerate liver tissue, reduce inflammation, and improve liver function, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used due to their high content of flavonoids and alkaloids, which have been found to have hepatoprotective and antihyperbilirubinemic properties.
Roots: Used due to their rich content of flavonoids and phenolic compounds, which have been found to have antioxidant and hepatoprotective effects.
Seeds: Used due to their high content of alkaloids and flavonoids, which have been found to have antihyperbilirubinemic and hepatoprotective effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest phyllanthus niruri leaves from a clean environment, using 50 grams of fresh leaves for the recipe.
Blend the harvested leaves with 250 milliliters of distilled water in a blender, for 30 seconds.
Steep the blended mixture in a heat-resistant container, under low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth, to obtain the herbal extract, and discard the solids.
Mix the herbal extract with 100 grams of a base cream, and 10 milliliters of a preservative, for a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Zingiber officinale creams helps with jaundice because of its anti-inflammatory properties and ability to stimulate digestion. The active compounds in ginger, such as gingerols and shogaols, help to reduce inflammation in the liver and promote the elimination of bilirubin, the pigment responsible for jaundice. Additionally, ginger has a natural warming effect that can help to stimulate bile production, further aiding in the reduction of jaundice symptoms. This makes ginger a potentially beneficial treatment for jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Gingerols: They help reduce inflammation and improve liver function, thereby aiding in the reduction of jaundice symptoms.
Shogaols: These compounds have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that help protect the liver from damage and improve its functioning.
Volatile oils (especially Zingiberene): They have hepatoprotective properties, which help protect the liver from damage and promote its recovery, ultimately reducing the severity of jaundice.
remedy_parts: Rhyzomes: The rhizomes of Zingiber officinale are the most used part due to their high concentration of active compounds, particularly gingerols and shogaols, which are known to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that help alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Roots: The roots of Zingiber officinale are also widely used for their ability to stimulate digestion, reduce nausea, and alleviate liver congestion associated with jaundice.

Buds: The buds of Zingiber officinale are used in traditional medicine to treat various liver and digestive issues, including jaundice, due to their potential to stimulate liver function and improve digestion.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 100g of fresh zingiber officinale rhizomes from mature plants in late summer or early fall.
Dry the harvested zingiber officinale rhizomes in a dehydrator at 50c for 8 hours or air-dry.
Grind 50g of dried zingiber officinale into a fine powder using a spice grinder for 30 seconds.
Combine 100g of coconut oil, 50g of beeswax, and 50g of powdered zingiber officinale in a double boiler.
Stir the mixture continuously at 60c for 20 minutes or until it reaches a smooth creamy consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Murraya koenigii creams helps with jaundice because they contain triterpenoid compounds, specifically koenigine, which has been found to have hepatoprotective properties. These compounds help to protect the liver from damage and promote the removal of bilirubin, a key factor in jaundice. Additionally, the anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties of Murraya koenigii creams may also help to soothe the liver and promote its natural healing processes, making them a potential natural remedy for jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Phenolics: These compounds help with jaundice by their antioxidant properties, which neutralize free radicals and reduce oxidative stress, thereby promoting liver health.
Flavonoids: Flavonoids in Murraya koenigii possess anti-inflammatory and hepatoprotective properties, which help in reducing liver inflammation and promoting liver function.
Coumarins: Coumarins in Murraya koenigii have been found to possess hepatoprotective properties, which help in protecting liver cells from damage and promoting liver recovery.
remedy_parts: Leaves: They are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce bilirubin levels in the body.
Roots: They contain bioactive compounds that help to promote liver function and reduce liver enzymes, which can help in treating jaundice.
Stems: They are also a good source of antioxidants and flavonoids that can help to reduce inflammation and promote liver health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 fresh murraya koenigii leaves at peak ripeness for optimal oil extraction and flavor.
Dry the murraya koenigii leaves in a low-temperature oven at 150°f for 6 hours to remove moisture.
Steep 1 tablespoon of dried murraya koenigii leaves in 2 tablespoons of carrier oil for 2 weeks to infuse the oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container to remove solids and debris.
Mix 10 grams of the herbal oil with 20 grams of emollient cream base and 5 grams of beeswax to create a smooth consistency.

remedies.remedy_desc: Artemisia absinthium creams helps with jaundice because they contain compounds like thujone and bornyl acetate, which have been traditionally used to stimulate liver function and improve bile flow. The antispasmodic and anti-inflammatory properties of these compounds may help reduce liver congestion and alleviate symptoms of jaundice, such as yellowing of the skin and eyes. Additionally, Artemisia absinthium creams may promote liver detoxification, helping to remove bilirubin and other toxins that contribute to jaundice.
remedy_constituents: Thujone: Helps to stimulate liver function and improve bile flow, which can alleviate jaundice symptoms.
Bornyl acetate: Exhibits hepatoprotective properties, which help to protect the liver from damage and promote its recovery.
Caffeic acid: Acts as an antioxidant and anti-inflammatory agent, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in the liver, which can contribute to jaundice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Known for their bitter compounds, which help stimulate liver function and relieve jaundice symptoms.
Roots: Used for their hepatoprotective properties, helping to protect the liver and reduce bilirubin levels in the blood.
Flowers: Rich in essential oils and flavonoids, which contribute to their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, beneficial in treating jaundice.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20-30 grams of dried artemisia absinthium leaves and flowers at dawn when essential oil content is highest.
Steep the dried herb in 200ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond or grapeseed oil for 2-3 weeks.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth or a coffee filter and discard the solids.
Add 10% beeswax to the infused oil and heat the mixture to 60-70 degrees celsius for 10 minutes.
Pour the hot mixture into small jars and allow it to cool and solidify completely before use.

remedies.remedy_desc: Eclipta alba creams helps with jaundice because of its rich content of sesquiterpene lactones, which have potent anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. These compounds help to reduce bilirubin levels in the liver, promoting its proper functioning and facilitating the breakdown of this pigment. Additionally, Eclipta alba creams may enhance liver regeneration and support its natural detoxification processes, ultimately aiding in the resolution of jaundice symptoms and promoting overall liver health. Its natural properties make it a promising alternative for jaundice treatment.
remedy_constituents: Flavonoids: These plant compounds help reduce bilirubin levels in the blood and liver, thereby alleviating jaundice symptoms.
Phenolic acids: These compounds possess antioxidant and hepatoprotective properties, which can help protect liver cells from damage and promote the breakdown of bilirubin.

Saponins: These glycosides have anti-inflammatory and immunomodulatory effects, which can help reduce liver inflammation and promote the regeneration of liver cells, ultimately aiding in the recovery from jaundice.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Used for their antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties to soothe and calm the skin.
Seeds: Used for their hepato-protective and antioxidant properties to support liver health and reduce jaundice symptoms.
Flowers: Used for their anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties to reduce inflammation and promote skin health.
remedy_recipe: Harvest eclipta alba flowers in the morning when they are dry and free of debris for 2 cups.
Infuse 2 cups of eclipta alba flowers in 4 cups of boiling water for 10 minutes.
Strain the infused liquid through a cheesecloth or a fine-mesh sieve into a clean container.
Mix the strained liquid with 1/4 cup of beeswax and 1/4 cup of coconut oil to create a smooth paste.
Whip the mixture until it thickens and becomes a smooth, spreadable cream in 10 minutes.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for jaundice are topical treatments derived from plants and herbs that help alleviate the symptoms of jaundice, a condition characterized by the buildup of bilirubin in the body, causing yellowing of the skin and eyes. The best herbal creams for jaundice include Cassia auriculata, Terminalia chebula, Curcuma longa, Silybum marianum, Glycyrrhiza glabra, Phyllanthus niruri, Zingiber officinale, Murraya koenigii, Artemisia absinthium, and Eclipta alba. If left untreated, jaundice can lead to liver damage, fatigue, and a weakened immune system, significantly impacting daily life, affecting work, social activities, and overall well-being.
